title: AutoHeal - User Input Protection: Focus Theft Detection & Auto-Recovery
desc: Multi-layer system protecting user focus from CDP/Chrome theft during ask operations. Layers: (1) prevFgHint early capture before EnsureCdpConnection, (2) SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE(8) not SW_MINIMIZE(6), (3) ForceForegroundWindow(AttachThreadInput) replacing silent-fail SetForegroundWindowRaw, (4) StartFocusWatchdog 200ms background loop during entire ask op, (5) RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync retry loop (150ms / 5s timeout) per step, (6) FocuslessWarningOverlay + ActionApi.OnFocusStealer prop stamp. RESTORE TRIGGER RULE: focus restore fires at the end of each ask/CDP action boundary -- not mid-action, not on a timer. Watchdog monitors only; actual restore is synchronous at action completion. CRITICAL GAP: after 5s restore timeout, system gives up silently.
steps: 0. [PRIORITY-1] RESTORE TRIGGER = end of each ask/CDP action. After every action (insert / send / file-attach / wait-response) completes: immediately call RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync before proceeding to next action. This is the ONLY sanctioned restore point -- do NOT restore mid-action or in watchdog tick.
1. prevFgGemini = GetForegroundWindow() BEFORE EnsureCdpConnection (not after tab switch)
2. EnsureCdpReadyAsync(prevFgHint) -- uses hint so EnsureCdp uses correct pre-switch prevFg
3. StartFocusWatchdog(prevFg, cdp) -- 200ms MONITOR ONLY: detect + notify, do NOT restore (restore is step 0 responsibility)
4. After insert-text action ends: GuardCdpFocusTheft -> RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
5. After file-attach action ends: LogRestoreFocus -> RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
6. After send action ends: LogRestoreFocus -> RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
7. After wait-response action ends: LogRestoreFocus -> RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
8. RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync: snapshot IME+cursor -> ForceForegroundWindow -> OnFocusStealer(AutoBugReport+overlay) -> retry loop -> restore IME+cursor
9. [TODO-P2] FireAutoHeal on restore timeout: after 5s exhausted add AutoBugReport(unrecoverable) + FireAutoHeal(focus-theft, chromeHwnd)
author: claude
